name: mocklify
description: AI-powered API mocking for VS Code. Design mock servers in plain English with GitHub Copilot, Claude, OpenAI, or Gemini; import OpenAPI/Swagger specs; simulate chaos and stateful CRUD flows; generate API documentation; and let Copilot agent mode drive your mocks.

commands.title: AI: Generate Mock Server from Description
AI: Generate Mock Server from Codebase
AI: Generate Mock Server from Recorded Traffic

chatParticipants.name: mocklify
chatParticipants.description: Design, document, and debug mock APIs with AI

commands.name: create
commands.description: Design a complete mock API server from a description

commands.name: route
commands.description: Generate and add routes to an existing mock server

commands.name: docs
commands.description: Generate polished API documentation for a mock server

commands.name: test
commands.description: Generate curl and REST Client test requests for a server

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze request logs: errors, unmatched requests, anomalies

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all mock servers and their status

commands.name: agent
commands.description: Agent mode: inspect and modify mock servers conversationally — every change asks first and can be undone

languageModelTools.name: mocklify_list_servers
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all Mocklify mock API servers in the workspace with their id, name, port, protocol, running status, base URL, and configured routes (method, path, status code). Use this first to discover servers before using other Mocklify tools.

languageModelTools.name: mocklify_create_server
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a new Mocklify mock API server. Returns the new server's id and port. The server starts empty (add routes with mocklify_add_route) and stopped (start it with mocklify_start_server).

port.description: Port to listen on (optional, defaults to the configured default port)

languageModelTools.name: mocklify_add_route
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Add a mock route to an existing Mocklify server. The route responds with the given JSON body and status code. Use Express-style paths with :param for path parameters (e.g. /api/users/:id). Provide realistic example data in responseBody.

server.description: Server id or name to add the route to

responseBody.description: JSON response body as a string, with realistic example data

languageModelTools.name: mocklify_start_server
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Start a Mocklify mock server so it begins serving its routes at http://localhost:<port>.

languageModelTools.name: mocklify_stop_server
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Stop a running Mocklify mock server.

languageModelTools.name: mocklify_get_request_logs
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get recent requests received by Mocklify mock servers: timestamp, method, path, response status, duration, and whether a mock route matched. Useful to debug why calls fail or to see what an app under test is requesting.

server.description: Server id or name (optional, all servers when omitted)

limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (default 25)

mocklify.autoStart.description: Automatically start servers when VS Code opens

mocklify.defaultPort.description: Default port for new Mocklify servers

mocklify.configPath.description: Path to configuration directory relative to workspace

mocklify.logging.maxEntries.description: Maximum number of log entries to keep

enumDescriptions: Use the first available provider (Copilot → Claude → OpenAI → Gemini)
GitHub Copilot (requires the GitHub Copilot extension)
Anthropic Claude (requires an API key — run 'Mocklify: Set AI Provider API Key')
mocklify.ai.provider.description: Which AI provider Mocklify uses for mock generation, documentation, and chat

mocklify.ai.copilotModel.markdownDescription: GitHub Copilot model family to use (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4.5`) — pick from the live list with **Mocklify: Select AI Model**. Leave empty to auto-select the best available model. Available families depend on your Copilot subscription.

mocklify.ai.claudeModel.markdownDescription: Claude model ID (pick from a list with **Mocklify: Select AI Model**). Anthropic API: `claude-opus-4-8`, `claude-sonnet-5`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`. Bedrock-compatible gateways use an `anthropic.` prefix, e.g. `anthropic.claude-opus-4-8`.

mocklify.ai.claudeBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Custom base URL for the Anthropic-compatible endpoint. Leave empty for the official Anthropic API. Set this when your company routes Claude through a gateway or proxy (e.g. a Bedrock-backed or LiteLLM gateway that speaks the Anthropic Messages API), e.g. `https://ai-gateway.mycompany.com`.

mocklify.ai.openaiModel.markdownDescription: OpenAI model ID (pick from a list with **Mocklify: Select AI Model**), e.g. `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4o-mini`, `gpt-4.1`.

mocklify.ai.openaiBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Custom base URL for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Leave empty for `https://api.openai.com/v1`. Set this for Azure OpenAI-compatible gateways, LiteLLM proxies, or other OpenAI-compatible servers, e.g. `https://ai-gateway.mycompany.com/v1`.

mocklify.ai.geminiModel.markdownDescription: Google Gemini model ID (pick from a list with **Mocklify: Select AI Model**), e.g. `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-pro`.

mocklify.ai.geminiBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Custom base URL for the Gemini-compatible endpoint. Leave empty for `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta`. Set this when routing Gemini through a corporate proxy or gateway.

enumDescriptions: Picks the best strategy per project: existing spec > agentic exploration > fast scan. Recommended.

One-shot analysis of heuristically extracted snippets. Cheapest and quickest. Escalates to agentic exploration only when no API patterns match anywhere in the workspace (and the provider supports tools).

The AI explores the codebase itself with read-only tools (reads files, follows imports to data models, finds auth/error conventions). Higher-quality routes, but slower (up to 8 minutes, 16 on multi-project workspaces) and uses substantially more AI tokens.
mocklify.ai.scanMode.markdownDescription: How **Mocklify: AI: Generate Mock Server from Codebase** analyzes the workspace. `auto` picks the best strategy per project: existing spec > agentic exploration > fast scan. `fast` forces one-shot analysis of heuristically extracted snippets (escalating to agentic exploration only when no API patterns match anywhere in the workspace); `agentic` forces AI exploration with read-only tools for deeper, higher-quality routes at more AI cost and time.

mocklify.ai.askQuestions.markdownDescription: Let the agentic codebase scan ask you short clarifying questions (at most 2 per API surface) when the code is genuinely ambiguous — shown as a QuickPick from the command flow or an inline card in the dashboard. An unanswered question times out after 2 minutes and the agent decides on its own, noting the assumption. Turn off for fully unattended scans.

mocklify.ai.driftWatch.markdownDescription: Watch saved source files for new API calls that no mock server covers and offer to generate routes (uses local scanning only — no AI calls until you accept).

mocklify.ai.driftNotifications.markdownDescription: Proactive drift notifications: when a saved source file calls API endpoints no mock server covers, show a notification whose **Fix in AI Chat** action opens the dashboard's AI chat pre-filled with a repair prompt — nothing is sent or changed until you press Send and approve the agent's proposed changes. Enabling this also enables on-save drift detection even when `#mocklify.ai.driftWatch#` is off. The same set of drifted endpoints is never re-notified within 30 minutes, and **Ignore for this session** mutes it until the window reloads.

mocklify.ai.scheduledScan.intervalMinute.markdownDescription: Re-scan the codebase with AI in the background every N minutes to keep scan memory fresh and surface newly discovered endpoints (respects `#mocklify.ai.scanMode#`). `0` disables scheduled scans (the default); values between 1 and 14 are treated as the 15-minute minimum. Background scans run fully unattended: they never ask questions, never create or modify servers, show progress only in the status bar, skip a cycle while another scan is running (or an interrupted scan is waiting to be resumed), and only notify — at most once per finding every 6 hours — when new uncovered endpoints or API surfaces turn up. Failures are logged and retried with backoff, never shown as error popups.

scripts.test:all: npm run test:unit && npm run compile:e2e && npm run test:e2e
scripts.release:cli: npm run build && npm run prepare:npm && cd npm-dist && npm publish
